title_en: The Prophet Muhammad and the Founding of the Islamic State
title_id: Nabi Muhammad ﷺ dan Pendirian Negara Islam
content_en:
Ibn al-Athir's Seerah section in Al-Kamil fit-Tarikh draws on the full body of earlier seerah and hadith literature that had accumulated in the three centuries between the Prophet's era and Ibn al-Athir's own time. By the sixth Islamic century, the biographical tradition of the Prophet was mature and extensive, and Ibn al-Athir had access to Ibn Hisham's recension of Ibn Ishaq's seerah, the major hadith collections of Bukhari and Muslim, and the specialized seerah and maghazi literature of scholars such as al-Waqidi and Ibn Sa'd. His treatment of the Seerah in Al-Kamil is more polished and narrative-focused than al-Tabari's isnad-heavy approach, presenting the life of the Prophet as a connected historical narrative with a clear arc from birth through prophethood to the establishment of the Islamic community and the final year before death.

The major events of the Meccan period, including the first revelation, the early converts, the persecutions, and the two emigrations to Abyssinia, are narrated with historical context that locates the early Islamic community within the political and social realities of Makkah. Ibn al-Athir gives attention to the opposition of the Quraysh leadership, the economic pressures applied through the boycott of Banu Hashim, and the significance of the conversions of Hamza ibn Abd al-Muttalib and 'Umar ibn al-Khattab as turning points in the early community's situation. The Hijra to Madinah is treated as the pivotal event in the transition from a persecuted minority to a community capable of building an Islamic state, and Ibn al-Athir conveys the historical significance of this moment without reducing it to mere political calculation.

The Madinan period receives systematic treatment through the major battles of Badr, Uhud, the Trench, and the subsequent expeditions and treaties that defined the political landscape of the Arabian Peninsula. Ibn al-Athir's accounts of these battles benefit from his broader historical perspective: he understands these campaigns not only as events in the Seerah but as the foundational military and diplomatic experiences that shaped the Islamic state's approach to expansion and governance. His assessment of the significance of specific battles and treaties is more evaluative than al-Tabari's, reflecting his role as a historian who synthesizes rather than merely archives. The conquest of Makkah in 8 AH, the Farewell Pilgrimage, and the death of the Prophet in 11 AH are all treated with appropriate solemnity and contextual richness.

A comparison of Ibn al-Athir's Seerah account with al-Tabari's reveals the trade-offs of his synthetic approach most clearly. Al-Tabari, working closer to the period, preserved more variant traditions and more raw source material. Ibn al-Athir, writing three centuries later, had the benefit of the full subsequent scholarly tradition including the systematic criticism of hadith chains that had clarified which narrations were reliable and which were problematic. His Seerah account is thus in some respects more critically refined than al-Tabari's, even as it is less comprehensive in its archival scope. For readers who want a reliable and readable account of the Prophet's life within the context of a universal Islamic history, Ibn al-Athir's treatment in Al-Kamil remains one of the most useful classical sources, capturing both the human drama and the theological significance of the founding prophetic period.
content_id:
Bagian sirah Ibn al-Athir dalam Al-Kamil fit-Tarikh mengandalkan seluruh kumpulan literatur sirah dan hadis awal yang telah terakumulasi selama tiga abad antara era Nabi ﷺ dan zaman Ibn al-Athir sendiri. Pada abad keenam Islam, tradisi biografis tentang Nabi ﷺ telah matang dan luas, dan Ibn al-Athir memiliki akses ke recension Ibn Hisham atas Sirah Ibn Ishaq, koleksi-koleksi hadis utama dari Imam Al-Bukhari dan Imam Muslim, serta literatur sirah dan maghazi khusus dari ulama-ulama seperti al-Waqidi dan Ibn Sa'd. Perlakuannya terhadap sirah dalam Al-Kamil lebih halus dan berfokus pada narasi daripada pendekatan al-Tabari yang sarat isnad, menyajikan kehidupan Nabi ﷺ sebagai narasi sejarah yang terhubung dengan alur yang jelas dari kelahiran melalui kenabian hingga pembentukan komunitas Islam dan tahun terakhir sebelum wafat.

Peristiwa-peristiwa besar periode Makkah, termasuk wahyu pertama, para mualaf awal, penganiayaan-penganiayaan, dan dua hijrah ke Habasyah, dinarasikan dengan konteks sejarah yang menempatkan komunitas Islam awal dalam realitas politik dan sosial Makkah. Ibn al-Athir memberikan perhatian pada oposisi pimpinan Quraisy, tekanan ekonomi yang diterapkan melalui pemboikotan Bani Hasyim, dan signifikansi masuk Islamnya Hamzah ibn Abd al-Muttalib dan Umar ibn al-Khattab (ra) sebagai titik balik dalam situasi komunitas awal. Hijrah ke Madinah diperlakukan sebagai peristiwa paling krusial dalam transisi dari minoritas yang dianiaya menjadi komunitas yang mampu membangun negara Islam, dan Ibn al-Athir menyampaikan signifikansi historis momen ini tanpa mereduksinya menjadi sekadar kalkulasi politik.

Periode Madinah mendapatkan perlakuan sistematis melalui peperangan-peperangan besar Badr, Uhud, Khandaq, serta ekspedisi-ekspedisi dan perjanjian-perjanjian berikutnya yang mendefinisikan lanskap politik Jazirah Arab. Kisah-kisah Ibn al-Athir tentang peperangan-peperangan ini mendapat manfaat dari perspektif historisnya yang lebih luas: beliau memahami kampanye-kampanye ini tidak hanya sebagai peristiwa dalam sirah, tetapi sebagai pengalaman militer dan diplomatik fundamental yang membentuk pendekatan negara Islam terhadap ekspansi dan tata kelola. Penilaiannya terhadap signifikansi pertempuran dan perjanjian tertentu lebih bersifat evaluatif daripada al-Tabari, mencerminkan perannya sebagai sejarawan yang mensintesis daripada sekadar mengarsipkan. Penaklukan Makkah pada tahun 8 AH, Haji Wada', dan wafatnya Nabi ﷺ pada tahun 11 AH semuanya diperlakukan dengan kesungguhan dan kekayaan kontekstual yang sesuai.

Perbandingan narasi sirah Ibn al-Athir dengan al-Tabari mengungkapkan pertukaran dalam pendekatan sintetisnya secara paling jelas. Al-Tabari, yang bekerja lebih dekat ke periode tersebut, melestarikan lebih banyak tradisi varian dan lebih banyak materi sumber mentah. Ibn al-Athir, yang menulis tiga abad kemudian, mendapat keuntungan dari seluruh tradisi keilmuan berikutnya termasuk kritik sistematis terhadap sanad hadis yang telah mengklarifikasi narasi mana yang dapat diandalkan dan mana yang bermasalah. Narasi sirahnya karenanya dalam beberapa hal lebih tersaring secara kritis daripada al-Tabari, meskipun lebih kurang komprehensif dalam cakupan arsipistiknya. Bagi pembaca yang menginginkan narasi kehidupan Nabi ﷺ yang dapat diandalkan dan mudah dibaca dalam konteks sejarah Islam universal, perlakuan Ibn al-Athir dalam Al-Kamil tetap menjadi salah satu sumber klasik yang paling berguna, menangkap baik drama kemanusiaan maupun signifikansi teologis dari periode kenabian yang mendirikan Islam.
content_ar: Ibn al-Athir's Seerah الفصل in Al-Kamil fit-Tarikh draws on the full الجسد of earlier seerah and الحديث literature that had accumulated in the three centuries between النبي ﷺ's era and Ibn al-Athir's own time. By the سادساً Islamic century, the biographical التراث of النبي ﷺ was mature and extensive, and Ibn al-Athir had access to Ibn Hisham's recension of Ibn Ishaq's seerah, the major الحديث collections of البخاري and مسلم, and the specialized seerah and maghazi literature of العلماء مثل al-Waqidi and Ibn Sa'd. His treatment of the Seerah in Al-Kamil is more polished and narrative-focused than al-Tabari's الإسناد-heavy approach, presenting the الحياة of النبي ﷺ as a connected historical narrative with a clear arc from birth through prophethood to the establishment of the Islamic الجماعة and the final year قبل الموت. The major events of the Meccan period, بما في ذلك the أولاً الوحي, the early converts, the persecutions, and the two emigrations to Abyssinia, are narrated with historical context that locates the early Islamic الجماعة within the political and social realities of مكة المكرمة. Ibn al-Athir gives attention to the opposition of the Quraysh leadership, the economic pressures applied through the boycott of Banu Hashim, and the significance of the conversions of Hamza ibn Abd al-Muttalib and 'عمر ibn al-Khattab as turning points in the early الجماعة's situation. The Hijra to المدينة المنورة is treated as the pivotal event in the transition from a persecuted minority to a الجماعة capable of building an Islamic state, and Ibn al-Athir conveys the historical significance of this moment without reducing it to mere political calculation. The Madinan period receives systematic treatment through the major battles of Badr, Uhud, the Trench, and the subsequent expeditions and treaties that defined the political landscape of the Arabian Peninsula. Ibn al-Athir's accounts of these battles benefit from his broader historical perspective: he understands these campaigns not only as events in the Seerah but as the foundational military and diplomatic experiences that shaped the Islamic state's approach to expansion and governance. His assessment of the significance of specific battles and treaties is more evaluative than al-Tabari's, reflecting his role as a historian who synthesizes rather than merely archives. The conquest of مكة المكرمة in 8 AH, the Farewell Pilgrimage, and the الموت of النبي ﷺ in 11 AH are all treated with appropriate solemnity and contextual richness.
